thesis_profile.market_mechanism: v96-A only-drop isolation experiment. Single-variable test versus v94 (xs_minimal_v9): drop the 4 weak factors per Phase 1d WF IC stability diagnostic, keep ALL 6 remaining factor weights EXACTLY as v94 (no reweighting, no rescue). Diagnostic-flagged weak factors removed: coinglass_top_trader_long_pct_smooth_5 (IC=-0.029, sign=56%), momentum_decay_5_20 (IC=-0.013, sign=47%), coinglass_taker_imb_intraday_dispersion_24h (IC=+0.023, sign=67%), quality_funding_oi (IC=-0.014, sign=59%). Compared to v95 (xs_minimal_v10) which dropped the same 4 weak factors AND added stress_liq_conc_iv AND used IC-proportional fresh weights, v96-A isolates only the drop component. v95 cycle showed WF sharpe regression 0.955 -> 0.675; v96-A cycle outcome will diagnose root cause: PASS means weak factors were genuinely noise (v95 regression came from stress_liq_conc_iv coverage or fresh weights); FAIL means weak factors provided portfolio-level stabilization the diagnostic missed (v94 is local optimum, further simplification regresses). v93 multiplier overlay preserved.
thesis_profile.directional_claim: rank top-3 liquid perp-covered names by 6 v94-weight z-score factors (volatility + structure + volume only; no derivatives factors), then scale all positions by DVOL regime multiplier. Expected if 4 weak factors were noise: rank IC ~0.18 (similar to v94 0.181), WF median sharpe ~0.95 (similar to v94 0.955), worst regime sharpe ~-2.7 (similar to v94 -2.73). Expected if 4 weak factors were stabilizers: WF sharpe drops below 0.85 (toward v95 territory), proving stabilization hypothesis. Falsification triggers v96-A rejection if WF median sharpe < 0.80 (significant regression vs v94 0.955) — same threshold as v95 manifest used.
thesis_profile.factor_formula: raw = -0.20*z(iv_smooth_20) -0.10*z(rv_5) +0.11*z(dh_60) +0.10*z(dh_5) +0.13*z(dh_20) -0.10*z(liquidity_stress_qv_iv); final = tanh((percentile_rank(raw)-0.5)*1.80); position_multiplier_overlay = clip(1 - tanh((max(btc_dvol_z90, eth_dvol_z90) - 0.5) * 3), 0.2, 1.0) applied at portfolio sizing layer (not in raw_score)
